item.name: Authentification

request.description:
## Register a New User

This endpoint registers a new user account in the system. It creates a user profile with the provided personal details and credentials.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/auth/register
```

---

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
|-----|-------|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as **raw JSON**.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `nom` | string | Last name of the user |
| `prenom` | string | First name of the user |
| `email` | string | User's email address (must be unique) |
| `password` | string | Account password |
| `password_confirmation` | string | Password confirmation — must match `password` |
| `date_naissance` | string | Date of birth in `YYYY-MM-DD` format |

**Example Body:**

```json
{
  "nom": "jane",
  "prenom": "smith",
  "email": "jane.smith@example.com",
  "password": "password123",
  "password_confirmation": "password123",
  "date_naissance": "1997-05-15"
}
```

---

### Authentication

> **No authentication required.** This endpoint is publicly accessible since it is used to create a new account.
>
> Note: Other endpoints in this collection use Bearer token authentication via `{{token}}`. Once registered, obtain a token through the login endpoint and set it as the `token` variable in your environment.

## Login

Authenticates an existing user and returns a JWT access token.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/auth/login
```

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Request Body

Send a JSON object with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| email | string | Yes | The registered email address of the user |
| password | string | Yes | The user's account password |

**Example:**

```json
{
  "email": "jane.smith@example.com",
  "password": "password123"
}
```

---

### Response

On success (`200 OK`), the response contains a JWT access token:

```json
{
  "access_token": "<jwt_token>"
}
```

The `access_token` is automatically saved to the `token` environment variable for use in subsequent authenticated requests.

---

### Authentication

This endpoint does **not** require authentication. It is used to obtain the bearer token needed for all other protected endpoints.

---

## Get Current User

Retrieves the profile information of the currently authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
GET {{url}}/users/me
```

---

### Authentication

This endpoint requires a valid Bearer token. The token is inherited from the collection-level auth configuration.

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Response

#### Success — `200 OK`

Returns the authenticated user's profile object.

```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "John Doe",
  "email": "john.doe@example.com",
  "phone": "+213xxxxxxxxx",
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "updated_at": "2024-06-01T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```

#### Error — `401 Unauthorized`

Returned when the Bearer token is missing or invalid.

```json
{
  "message": "Unauthenticated."
}
```

---

### Notes

- The `{{token}}` variable must be set in the active environment (`RestApi`) before sending this request.
- This endpoint always returns the profile of the user associated with the provided token, regardless of any user ID in the URL.

## Logout

Logs out the currently authenticated user by invalidating their session or access token.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/auth/logout
```

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Authentication

This endpoint requires a valid Bearer token to be included in the request. The token is inherited from the collection-level auth configuration.

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Request Body

The request body should be sent as raw JSON.

```json
{
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "password": "secret123"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| email | string | The email address of the user logging out |
| password | string | The password of the user |

---

### Responses

| Status Code | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| 200 OK | User successfully logged out |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid or expired token |
| 422 Unprocessable Entity | Validation error in request body |

---

### Notes

- After a successful logout, the token associated with the user session is invalidated and can no longer be used for authenticated requests.
- Ensure the `{{token}}` environment variable is set before calling this endpoint.

## Update Current User

Updates the profile of the currently authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
PUT {{url}}/users/me
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|---|---|
| Content-Type | application/json |

---

### Request Body

Send a JSON object with the fields you want to update:

```json
{
  "nom": "John Updated"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `nom` | string | The new name to assign to the current user |

---

### Response

**200 OK** — The user profile was successfully updated. The response body contains the updated user object reflecting the new values.

## Update Password

Updates the password of the currently authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
PATCH {{url}}/users/me/password
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. The token must be included via the collection-level auth configuration using `{{token}}`.

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Request Body

Send a JSON object with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `current_password` | string | Yes | The user's current password |
| `new_password` | string | Yes | The new password to set |
| `new_password_confirmation` | string | Yes | Must match `new_password` |

**Example:**

```json
{
  "current_password": "password123",
  "new_password": "newpassword456",
  "new_password_confirmation": "newpassword456"
}
```

---

### Response

Returns a success message upon a valid password change. Returns an error if the current password is incorrect or the new passwords do not match.

item.description:
## Authentification & User Profile Management

This folder groups all endpoints related to user authentication and profile management. It covers the full authentication lifecycle — from account creation and login to session termination — as well as operations for viewing and updating the authenticated user's profile.

---

### Covered Operations

- **Register** — Create a new user account with personal details and credentials.
    
- **Login** — Authenticate with email and password to receive a JWT access token.
    
- **Get Current User** — Retrieve the profile of the currently authenticated user.
    
- **Update Profile** — Modify the authenticated user's profile information.
    
- **Update Password** — Change the authenticated user's password securely.
    
- **Logout** — Invalidate the current session token to end the user's session.
    

---

### Authentication

Most endpoints in this folder require a valid Bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

The `{{token}}` variable is automatically populated after a successful **Login** request and is inherited from the collection-level auth configuration. The **Register** and **Login** endpoints are publicly accessible and do not require a token.

item.name: Accounts

request.description:
## Create Courant Account

Creates a new Courant (current/checking) bank account for the authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/accounts
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. The token is automatically applied via the collection-level auth configuration.

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Authorization | Bearer {{token}} |

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as JSON.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | string | Yes | Account type. Must be `"Courant"` |
| `balance` | integer | Yes | Initial balance for the account |
| `name` | string | No | Optional display name for the account |

**Example:**

```json
{
  "type": "Courant",
  "balance": 1000
}
```

---

### Response

On success, returns the newly created account object with its details.

---

### Notes

- The `type` field must be set to `"Courant"` to create a standard current account.
- Make sure the `{{url}}` and `{{token}}` environment variables are set in the active environment before sending the request.

## Create Account (Épargne)

Creates a new savings (Épargne) bank account for the currently authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/accounts

 ```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. The token must be included via the collection-level auth configuration:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as raw JSON.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | string | Yes | Account type. Must be `"EPARGNE"` for a savings account. Possible values: `EPARGNE`, `COURANT`, `MINEUR` |
| `balance` | number | Yes | Initial deposit balance for the account |
| `interest_rate` | number | Yes | Annual interest rate applied to the savings account |
| `daily_transfer_limit` | number | Yes | Maximum amount that can be transferred per day |

**Example Body:**

``` json
{
  "type": "EPARGNE",
  "balance": 5000,
  "interest_rate": 3.5,
  "daily_transfer_limit": 1000
}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, returns the newly created account object with its assigned ID and details.

---

### Notes

- The `EPARGNE` account type is a savings account that accrues interest based on the provided `interest_rate`.
    
- The `daily_transfer_limit` restricts the total outgoing transfers allowed per day from this account.

## Create Account Mineur

Creates a new minor (Mineur) bank account for the authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/accounts
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. The token is inherited from the collection level.

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Request Body

Send a JSON payload with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | string | Yes | Account type. Must be `"Mineur"` for a minor account. |
| `balance` | integer | Yes | Initial balance for the account (e.g., `1000`). |

**Example:**

```json
{
  "type": "Mineur",
  "balance": 1000
}
```

---

### Response

On success, returns the newly created account object with its details.

---

### Notes

- A **Mineur** account is a bank account designated for minors.
- Make sure the `{{url}}` and `{{token}}` environment variables are set in the active environment before sending this request.

## Get All Accounts

Retrieves a list of all bank accounts associated with the currently authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
GET {{url}}/accounts
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. The token is inherited from the collection-level auth configuration.

| Header | Value |
|---|---|
| Authorization | Bearer {{token}} |

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|---|---|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Response

On success, the endpoint returns an HTTP `200 OK` status with a JSON array of account objects belonging to the authenticated user.

**Example Response:**

```json
[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "type": "savings",
    "balance": 15000.00,
    "currency": "DZD",
    "owner": {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "John Doe"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "type": "current",
    "balance": 3200.50,
    "currency": "DZD",
    "owner": {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "John Doe"
    }
  }
]
```

---

### Notes

- Requires a valid Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
- Returns only accounts owned by or associated with the authenticated user.

## Get Account

Retrieves the details of a specific bank account by its unique ID.

### Request

**Method:** `GET`  
**URL:** `{{url}}/accounts/1`

### URL Notes

The `1` at the end of the URL represents the **account ID**. To retrieve a different account, replace `1` with the desired account's ID.

### Variables

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `{{url}}` | The base URL of the API server |
| `{{token}}` | The authentication token used for Bearer authorization |

### Authentication

This request requires a **Bearer token** for authentication. The `{{token}}` variable should contain a valid token, which is sent in the `Authorization` header as:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

### Expected Behavior

Returns the full details of the bank account with ID `1`, including account information such as account number, balance, owner details, and status.

## Add Member

Adds a co-owner (member) to a specific bank account by providing the target user's ID. This allows multiple users to share ownership of a bank account.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/accounts/2/co-owners

 ```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required for this request. Include the token using the `{{token}}` variable, which must be set in your active environment.

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

---

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Request Body

Send a raw JSON body containing the ID of the user to be added as a co-owner.

``` json
{
  "user_id": 2
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `user_id` | integer | The ID of the user to add as a co-owner of the account. |

---

### Response

On success, the API returns a `200 OK` or `201 Created` status code along with a JSON object confirming the newly added co-owner's details and their association with the account.

## List Co-Owners of an Account

Retrieves the list of co-owners (members) associated with a specific bank account. This endpoint returns all users who have shared access to the given account.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
GET {{url}}/accounts/1/co-owners
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. Include the `{{token}}` variable in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `1` | Integer | Yes | The unique identifier of the bank account whose co-owners are being retrieved. |

---

### Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies that the response should be returned in JSON format. |

---

### Response

Returns a list of co-owner objects associated with the specified account. Each object contains details about a co-owner who has shared access to the account.

**Success Response — `200 OK`**

```json
[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john.doe@example.com"
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "name": "Jane Smith",
    "email": "jane.smith@example.com"
  }
]
```

## Remove Member (Co-Owner) from Account

Removes a co-owner (member) from a specific bank account. This action permanently revokes the co-owner's access to the account.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `accountId` | integer | Yes | The unique identifier of the bank account from which the co-owner will be removed. |
| `coOwnerId` | integer | Yes | The unique identifier of the co-owner (member) to be removed from the account. |

**Example URL:** `{{url}}/accounts/2/co-owners/2`

---

### Authentication

This endpoint requires a **Bearer Token** for authorization.

Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

Ensure the `{{token}}` variable is set in your active environment before sending the request.

---

### Response

| Status Code | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `200 OK` | The co-owner was successfully removed. A confirmation response body may be returned. |
| `204 No Content` | The co-owner was successfully removed. No response body is returned. |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Authentication failed. Check that your `{{token}}` is valid and not expired. |
| `403 Forbidden` | You do not have permission to remove a co-owner from this account. |
| `404 Not Found` | The specified account or co-owner was not found. |

## Assign Guardien

Assigns a guardian to a specific bank account by its ID. A guardian is a user who is granted oversight or supervisory access to the account. This endpoint links the specified user as the guardian of the given account.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/accounts/1/guardian
```

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter  | Type    | Required | Description                                      |
|------------|---------|----------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `id`       | integer | Yes      | The unique identifier of the bank account (e.g., `1`) |

---

### Authentication

Bearer token required. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

The `{{token}}` variable is inherited from the collection-level authentication configuration.

---

### Request Headers

| Header   | Value              | Description                          |
|----------|--------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies that the response should be in JSON format |

---

### Request Body

Send a raw JSON object with the following field:

```json
{
  "user_id": 2
}
```

| Field     | Type    | Required | Description                                                  |
|-----------|---------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `user_id` | integer | Yes      | The unique identifier of the user to be assigned as guardian of the account |

---

### Notes & Behavior

- The account must exist and belong to the authenticated user before a guardian can be assigned.
- Only one guardian can be assigned per account at a time. Calling this endpoint again may replace the existing guardian.
- The user referenced by `user_id` must be a valid, registered user in the system.
- A successful request returns a confirmation response indicating the guardian has been assigned.

## Convert Account

Converts an existing bank account to a different account type (e.g., from a minor/Mineur account to a standard account).

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
PATCH {{url}}/accounts/3/convert

 ```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. The token is inherited from the collection level.

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Yes | The unique ID of the account to convert. |

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `Accept` | `application/json` |

---

### Response

On success, the endpoint returns the updated account object reflecting the new account type.

---

### Notes

- Only accounts eligible for conversion can be targeted by this endpoint.
    
- The authenticated user must be the owner of the account.

## Delete Account

Permanently deletes a specific bank account by its ID. Once deleted, the account and all associated data cannot be recovered.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
DELETE {{url}}/accounts/2
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token authentication is required. The token must be included via the collection-level authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type    | Required | Description                          |
|-----------|---------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| `id`      | integer | Yes      | The unique ID of the account to delete. (e.g., `2`) |

---

### Request Body

No request body is required for this endpoint.

---

### Response

#### Success — `200 OK` or `204 No Content`

The account was successfully deleted. The response may be empty or contain a confirmation message.

#### Error — `401 Unauthorized`

The request is missing a valid authentication token.

#### Error — `403 Forbidden`

The authenticated user does not have permission to delete this account.

#### Error — `404 Not Found`

No account was found with the specified ID.

---

### Notes

- This action is **irreversible**. Ensure the correct account ID is provided before sending the request.
- Only the account owner or an authorized user can perform this operation.

## Closure Consent

Records or updates a customer's consent to close their bank account. This endpoint is used to capture the account holder's explicit agreement before proceeding with the account closure process.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
PATCH {{url}}/accounts/1/closure-consent
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token required in Authorization header (inherited from collection):

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | integer | Yes | The unique ID of the account for which closure consent is being recorded |

---

### Response

#### Success (200 OK)

Returns a confirmation that the closure consent has been successfully recorded or updated for the specified account.

#### Error Responses

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| 401 | Unauthorized — missing or invalid token |
| 403 | Forbidden — insufficient permissions |
| 404 | Account not found |
| 422 | Unprocessable Entity — invalid consent data |

## Transfer Funds

Initiates a money transfer from one bank account to another within the AlMadar Bank system.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
POST {{url}}/transfers
```

---

### Authentication

This endpoint requires a valid Bearer token. The token is automatically applied via the collection-level auth configuration.

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Accept | application/json |

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as raw JSON.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `sender_id` | integer | Yes | The ID of the account sending the funds |
| `receiver_id` | integer | Yes | The ID of the account receiving the funds |
| `amount` | integer | Yes | The amount to transfer (in the account's currency) |
| `reason` | string | Yes | A short description or reason for the transfer |

**Example Body:**

```json
{
  "sender_id": 1,
  "receiver_id": 2,
  "amount": 250,
  "reason": "Rent payment"
}
```

---

### Responses

| Status Code | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| `201 Created` | Transfer was successfully initiated |
| `400 Bad Request` | Invalid or missing fields in the request body |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Missing or invalid authentication token |
| `403 Forbidden` | The authenticated user does not have permission to perform this transfer |
| `404 Not Found` | Sender or receiver account not found |
| `422 Unprocessable Entity` | Business rule violation (e.g. insufficient funds) |

## List Transfers

Retrieves a list of all transfers associated with the currently authenticated user.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
GET {{url}}/transfers
```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token required in the Authorization header.

item.description:
## Accounts

This folder contains all endpoints related to bank account management in the AlMadar Bank API. It covers the full lifecycle of an account — from creation and retrieval to membership management, type conversion, and deletion.

### Endpoints Overview

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/accounts` | Create a new **Courant** (current/checking) account |
| `POST` | `/accounts` | Create a new **Mineur** (minor) account |
| `GET` | `/accounts` | Retrieve all accounts for the authenticated user |
| `GET` | `/accounts/{id}` | Retrieve details of a specific account by ID |
| `POST` | `/accounts/{id}/co-owners` | Add a co-owner (member) to an account |
| `DELETE` | `/accounts/{id}/co-owners/{coOwnerId}` | Remove a co-owner (member) from an account |
| `PATCH` | `/accounts/{id}/convert` | Convert an account to a different type |
| `DELETE` | `/accounts/{id}` | Permanently delete a specific account |

### Authentication

All requests in this folder require **Bearer Token** authentication, inherited from the collection level. Ensure the `{{token}}` and `{{url}}` environment variables are set in your active environment before sending any request.

item.name: Admin

request.description:
## Get All Bank Accounts

Retrieves a list of all bank accounts. This is an **admin-only** endpoint intended for administrative operations and oversight of account data.

---

### Endpoint

| Method | URL |
|--------|-----|
| `GET` | `{{url}}/admin/accounts` |

---

### Authentication

This endpoint requires a **Bearer Token** for authorization. The token is supplied via the `{{token}}` variable from the active environment.

Include the following header in your request:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

Ensure the `token` variable is set in your environment (e.g., `RestApi`) before sending the request.

---

### Notes

- This endpoint is restricted to **admin users** only. Requests made with non-admin credentials will be rejected.
- The `url` variable should be set to the base URL of the API server in your active environment.
- No request body is required for this endpoint.
- The response will contain a list of bank account records managed by the system.

## Block User

Blocks a specific user account by its ID. Once blocked, the account holder will be unable to perform transactions or access banking services until the account is unblocked by an administrator.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
PATCH {{url}}/admin/accounts/1/block
```

---

### Authentication

This endpoint requires a valid Bearer token. The token must belong to an **admin** user.

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter  | Type    | Required | Description                          |
|------------|---------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| `1`        | integer | Yes      | The ID of the account to be blocked  |

---

### Response

**Success (200 OK)**

Returns a confirmation that the account has been successfully blocked.

---

### Notes

- Only admin users can perform this action.
- Blocking an account does not delete it; it can be unblocked later using the Unblock User endpoint.
- The `{{url}}` variable should be set in your active environment.

## Unblock User

Unblocks a previously blocked user account by its ID. Once unblocked, the account holder will regain the ability to perform transactions and access banking services.

---

### HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
PATCH {{url}}/admin/accounts/1/unblock

 ```

---

### Authentication

Bearer token required. Include the token in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| id | integer | Yes | The unique ID of the account to unblock |

---

### Response

Returns a success message or the updated account status upon successful unblocking.

item.description:
## Admin

This folder contains **admin-only** endpoints for managing bank accounts within the AlMadar Bank system. All requests require a valid Bearer token belonging to an administrator.

### Endpoints Overview

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `{{url}}/admin/accounts` | Retrieve a list of all bank accounts |
| `PATCH` | `{{url}}/admin/accounts/{id}/block` | Block a specific user account by ID |
| `PATCH` | `{{url}}/admin/accounts/{id}/unblock` | Unblock a previously blocked user account by ID |

### Authentication

All endpoints in this folder require a Bearer token passed in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

### Notes

- These operations are restricted to **admin users** only.
- Blocking an account suspends the user's ability to perform transactions and access banking services.
- Blocking is reversible — a blocked account can be restored at any time using the **Unblock User** endpoint.
- Ensure the `url` and `token` variables are configured in your active environment before sending requests.
